pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals NOAA website - verified each chemical exists and has documented properties on https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/
CAMEO Chemicals data analysis: DMSO is the only solvent that passes all three criteria: (1) Flash Point 203°F > 140°F; (2) Water Solubility ≥100 mg/mL (Miscible); (3) Not listed as carcinogen
CAMEO Chemicals and PubChem recognize DMSO as standard acronym for Dimethyl Sulfoxide

pi_items.verified_value: Dimethyl Sulfoxide (contains two methyl groups, not one)
pi_items.source: Chemical nomenclature: DMSO is technically 'dimethyl sulfoxide' (two methyl groups). 'Methyl sulfoxide' is an approximation used colloquially but not the precise chemical name. Verified via PubChem and chemical sources.

privileged_info_summary: The task evaluates five candidate solvents (Benzene, Acetone, Dimethyl Sulfoxide, Methylene Chloride, Acetophenone) against three criteria from CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA): Fire Safety (Flash Point > 140°F or non-flammable), Process Efficiency (Water Solubility must be Soluble/Miscible/Complete), and Health Safety (no carcinogen listing). Data from CAMEO Chemicals confirms Dimethyl Sulfoxide is the correct winner: it has Flash Point 203°F (passing fire safety), is miscible in water (passing process efficiency), and is not listed as a carcinogen (passing health safety). All four other candidates fail at least one criterion. Benzene fails all three criteria. Acetone fails fire safety (Flash Point 0°F). Methylene Chloride fails health safety (known carcinogen) and process efficiency (slightly soluble). Acetophenone fails process efficiency (data unavailable/slightly soluble). The data source is CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), a real NOAA public database, not synthetic data.
